data.title: India's Sensex Opens 1.3% Higher at 74835.49 on Broad-Based Gains
Argenx Gains US FDA Approval for Self-injectable Vyvgart Hytrulo Syringe
data.description:
Biotechnology company Argenx said Thursday it secured the US Food and Drug Administration's approval for its self-injectable prefilled syringe of Vyvgart Hytrulo.

The drug is a treatment for adults with chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy and generalized myasthenia gravis, who are anti-acetylcholine receptor antibody positive.

The regulator's approval was based on studies demonstrating the self-injected form of Vyvgart Hytrulo is bioequivalent to the drug in a vial. Additionally, human factors studies showed that patients and caregivers could safely prepare and administer Vyvgart Hytrulo with a prefilled syringe.

data.title: ICYMI - Harley-Davidson board member resigns, cites 'grave concerns' about company
data.description:
Info via Reuters.

A Harley-Davidson board member has resigned after raising serious concerns about the company's direction, triggering an 11% drop in the stock on Thursday.

Jared Dourdeville, who represents Harley's second-largest shareholder, H Partners, cited “cultural depletion” stemming from leadership departures and remote work policies in a letter to the board. He also called for the resignation of CEO Jochen Zeitz and two other directors.

Harley said Dourdeville quit ahead of a scheduled board meeting to address his concerns and after voting against the board’s decision not to appoint his preferred CEO candidate.

Earlier this week, Harley announced that Zeitz plans to retire in 2025, staying on until a successor is found. The company continues to face challenges attracting younger riders, and is now leaning on higher-margin Touring bikes and custom vehicle offerings to appeal to wealthier customers.

Bit of an old pic, but they don't change too much

data.title: Agri Picks Report April 11, 2025: Geojit Financial Services
data.description:
Geojit Financial Services's report on Daily Agri Picks

Private weather forecaster Skymet has predicted a 'normal' southwest monsoon for India at 103% of the longperiod average, with an error margin of +/-5%. The season is likely to begin with relatively less rains, as the forecaster predicts rainfall in June at 96% of the long-period average. However, it is expected to increase from July, with rainfall during the month seen at 102%, that in August at 108%, and in September at 104%. According to Skymet Managing Director Jatin Singh, this season, the La Nina weather phenomenon has been weak and brief. "Vital signs of La Nina have started fading now. However, the occurrence of El Nino, which normally corrupts the monsoon, has been ruled out," he said in a statement. El Nino–Southern Oscillation-neutral is likely to be the most dominant category during the Indian summer monsoon, Singh said. The remnants of La Nina and ENSO-neutral together are likely to shield the monsoon from any egregious outcome. El Nino-Southern Oscillation refers to the fluctuation between El Nino and La Nina conditions in the Pacific Ocean. El Nino is linked to drier weather in India, while La Nina results in more favourable rainfall. The preliminary forecast of a positive Indian Ocean Dipole will work towards better monsoon prospects. The Indian Ocean Dipole is the difference in sea surface temperatures between the eastern and western tropical Indian Ocean. A negative Indian Ocean Dipole leads to less rainfall in India and vice versa. The prediction of another normal monsoon is likely to boost hope of robust kharif sowing in the coming season. India receives about 75% of its annual rainfall during the Jun-Sept period, making it crucial for sowing activities as well as reservoir replenishment.

data.title: Senegal Consumer Prices Fall 0.2% in March
data.description:
Consumer prices in Senegal fell 0.2% year-on-year in March 2025, following a 0.6% increase in the previous month.

This marks the first decline in four periods, mainly driven by lower prices for food and non-alcoholic beverages (-0.9%) and information and communication goods and services (-8.1%).

These declines were partially offset by price increases in other categories, particularly restaurants and accommodation services (+1.6%), furniture, household equipment, and routine maintenance (+1.7%), housing, water, electricity, gas, and other fuels (+0.9%), and transport (+1.0%).

Excluding energy and fresh products, the core index rose by 2.1% year-on-year.

On a monthly basis, the CPI fell 0.5%, after a 0.6% decline in February.

data.title: Trump's Tariff Pause Likely to Bring Relief to Asian EquitiesMarket Talk
data.description: President Trump's 90-day tariff pause should bring relief for Asian equities, excluding China and Hong Kong, Nomura analyst Chetan Seth says in a research note. The pause suggests that "we might have already crossed the point of peak-pessimism" and that "there is indeed some pain threshold for the Trump administration," the analyst notes. However, Hong Kong and China equities are still not off the hook yet and will likely lag within the region, as U.S.-China trade tensions continue to rise, he says. Nomura expects volatility in the next 90 days, as there will be back-and-forth trade negotiations. If a U.S. recession is eventually avoided, the risk-reward appears attractive for Asian equities, excluding Japan, with only limited downside in general, he adds. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Dutch Group SBM Offshore Secures $1.1 Billion Revolver
data.description:
SBM Offshore secured a $1.1 billion revolving credit facility from a group of 13 international banks, according to a Thursday release.

The new five-year unsecured facility offers two one-year extensions and an uncommitted option to upsize by $500 million. It was signed to refinance the Dutch deepwater ocean infrastructure company's current $1 billion facility maturing in February 2026.

SBM Offshore will use the funds for general corporate purposes and working capital needs, with a $100 million tranche allocated to eligible green projects.

data.title: Novartis Plans $23 Billion Boost for US Manufacturing, Research
data.description:
Novartis said Thursday it plans to invest $23 billion to expand its manufacturing, research and development footprint in the US over the next five years.

The Swiss pharmaceutical company's proposal involves a new biomedical research innovation hub in San Diego, two new radioligand therapy manufacturing facilities in Florida and Texas, and the expansion of three RLT manufacturing facilities in Indiana, New Jersey and California.

The plan includes four new manufacturing facilities, the locations of which will be determined later. The sites will produce biologics drug substances, chemical drug substances, drug products, device assembly and packaging.

data.title: Carawine Resources Signs Loan Agreement With Major Shareholder
data.description:
Carawine Resources entered into an unsecured loan facility agreement with its major shareholder, QGold, for AU$1.7 million, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The loan has a term of 18 months after the first drawdown, with a fixed interest rate of 10% per annum, payable at the end of the term, the filing said.

The proceeds will fund the completion of a heritage survey at the Paterson project in Western Australia and maintain exploration project tenure, staffing, and support activities until September, per the filing.

data.title: ABB Launches $40 Million US Manufacturing Plant for Cable Accessories, Circuit Solutions
data.description:
ABB's ABB Installation Products opened an over $40 million manufacturing facility for cable accessories and circuit solutions in Albuquerque, in the US state of New Mexico.

The Swiss-Swedish electrification and automation group said Thursday the 90,000-square-foot greenfield facility affirms its commitment to supporting critical utility projects and infrastructure improvements in the US.

Over the last five years, ABB Installation Products has spent over $125 million on expansions and improvements across the US. The company also earmarked $15 million for equipment upgrades and automation.

data.title: Geely Automobile at one-week high on robust earnings outlook
data.description:
** Shares of Geely Automobile Holdings rise 4.8% to HK$15.64, their highest levels since April 3

** Stock on track for a fourth consecutive session of gains, if current trend holds

** Stock set for the biggest one-day pct gain since February 27; third biggest pct gainer on Hang Seng Index , which slips 0.7%

** Geely expects its profit attributable for the three-month period ended March to be in the range of 5.2 bln to 5.8 bln yuan ($710.13-$792.07 mln), up 220%-270% y/y, due to record-high sales volume and robust growth in its new energy business

** UOB Kay Hian maintains "market weight" on autombile with top BUYs including BYD, Geely and Desay SV

** U.S. tariffs on Chinese autos (up to 150%) have limited direct impact but raise global trade tensions, posing risks to demand and earnings - UOB

** Hang Seng Commerce & Industry Index eases 0.9%, Hang Seng Tech Index slips 0.3%

** Stock up 4.2%, YTD

($1 = 7.3226 Chinese yuan)

data.title: From Joke To Juggernaut: Dogecoin Value Revolution Gets Nod From Global Asset Giant
data.description:
Swiss asset manager 21Shares has openly endorsed Dogecoin, stating that the cryptocurrency has developed a long way from its origins as an online meme. The company cited Dogecoin’s whopping 130,000% price appreciation over the last decade as evidence of its longevity within the turbulent crypto space.

Meme Currency Sees Serious Growth

What began as an online joke in 2013 has become what 21Shares refers to as a “movement” in the crypto space. Dogecoin’s performance, the asset manager says, speaks for itself. The coin has recorded an annual growth rate of 125% since its inception, making it the best performer among the market’s top 25 largest cryptocurrencies by market cap.

The growth is not just in value. User adoption has nearly doubled in recent years, with wallet addresses rising from 44 million to 84 million in four years. Such rapid growth shows that more people are holding and using the cryptocurrency despite its lighthearted origin.

21Shares
@21Shares

Dogecoin isn’t just a meme—it’s a movement.

With 130,000%+ returns, a $30B market cap, and 84M+ wallets, DOGE is rewriting what value means in the digital age.

Explore how culture, community, and memes drive this phenomenon. Read the full blog → https://t.co/wNFYdM2pjS pic.twitter.com/ojfYEkVCwQ

Apr 10, 2025

ETF Filing Marks Major Step For Dogecoin

The Swiss company recently submitted an S-1 form to the US Securities and Exchange Commission for a Dogecoin ETF. The filing is a significant step forward for cryptocurrency, which began life as a prank. If accepted, the ETF would allow ordinary investors to own exposure to Dogecoin without buying or owning the cryptocurrency itself.

According to regulatory filings, the new fund would be commodity-based, providing an alternative method of bringing Dogecoin into portfolios using traditional investment vehicles.21Shares Announces Partnership With Dogecoin Foundation

21Shares also announced that it has partnered with the House of Doge, the official business entity of the Dogecoin Foundation. From reports, the partnership is said to further entrench Dogecoin with conventional financial systems.

The alliance brings a new legitimacy to the currency, with traditional financial institutions now viewing it as a legitimate asset class and not merely an internet fad. Institutional support may entice more risk-averse investors who shunned the meme-coin in the past.New Exchange-Traded Product Launches With Physical Backing

In a further demonstration of its dedication to Dogecoin, 21Shares has introduced an exchange-traded product fully supported by the Dogecoin Foundation. This investment product will be collateralized by real Dogecoin in a 1:1 ratio, such that every share equates to holding real cryptocurrency in cold storage.

The firm will charge a management fee of 0.25% for this product, which is fairly competitive against peer cryptocurrency investment products. This physical backing model provides investors with confidence that their investment holds a real-world basis in the form of coins and not synthetic derivatives.

Featured image from Unsplash, chart from TradingView

data.description:

data.title: Market Chatter: Japan Forms Trade Task Force Amid US Negotiations; Considers Tax Relief
data.description:
Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba established a US trade negotiation task force led by Economy Minister Ryosei Akazawa, who plans to visit Washington next week, as lawmakers urge measures to counter potential US tariff impacts, according to a Nikkei Asia report on Friday.

The ruling coalition is weighing temporary food tax cuts to ease household burdens, with Komeito leader Tetsuo Saito advocating for direct relief through tax reductions and cash payouts, said the news outlet.

However, some LDP members oppose sales tax cuts, citing their importance for funding Japan's aging society, even as food prices climb under the current 8%-10% tax structure, the report said.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Montage Technology More than Triples 2024 Profit; Shares Up 6%
data.description:
Montage Technology posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 1.41 billion yuan, up 213% from 450.9 million yuan the previous year.

Earnings per share jumped to 1.25 yuan from 0.40 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The integrated circuit design company's revenue rose 59% year over year to 3.64 billion yuan from 2.29 billion yuan.

Shares of Montage Technology rose 6% in recent trade.

data.title: China's yuan rebounds from 2007 lows, but slides against trading partners
data.description:
Key points:
  • Yuan slips to 19-month low against trading partners
  • PBOC wary of sharp yuan declines
  • Analysts see limited impact of yuan weakening on tariffs

China's yuan bounced off a 2007 trough against a broadly weaker dollar on Friday, but it slipped to a 19-month low against currencies of its major trading partners, as a trade row between the world's two largest economies showed few signs of abating.

Selling resumed in financial markets after U.S. President Donald Trump's temporary pause on tariffs for many countries sparked a brief rally a day earlier.

Trump's 90-day respite, despite his insistence for days that his policies would never change, didn't include China. Instead, he ratcheted up duties on Chinese imports to an effective 145% rate.

The trade war comes at a challenging time for China as policymakers have struggled to revitalise economic growth after the COVID-19 slump, weighed down by a protracted property market downturn and deflationary forces.

However, the People's Bank of China will not allow sharp yuan declines and has instructed major state-owned lenders to reduce dollar purchases, people with knowledge of the matter told Reuters.

A weaker yuan would make Chinese exports cheaper and alleviate tariff impact on the economy. However, a sharp decline could also increase unwanted capital outflows and risk financial stability, analysts and economists said.

To reflect the broad dollar weakness in global markets, the PBOC lifted its official yuan midpoint guidance fix for the first time in seven days on Friday.

Prior to the market open, the PBOC set the rate , around which the yuan is allowed to trade in a 2% band, at 7.2087 per dollar.

That was 5 pips firmer than the previous fix and 1,017 pips firmer than a Reuters' estimate of 7.3104.

The PBOC has slightly loosened its grip on the currency this week by allowing official guidance to weaken past the key threshold of 7.2 to the dollar, although it came in much stronger than market projections, in what traders and analysts interpreted as an official attempt to keep the yuan steady.

The onshore yuan rebounded from a trough of 7.3518 per dollar hit a day earlier, the level that was last seen during global financial crisis, to 7.3160 around 0212 GMT. But it remained down 0.8% this month.

Its offshore counterpart was at 7.3224 as of 0212 GMT, down 0.12% in Asian trade. It hit an all-time low of 7.4288 on Tuesday.

The CFETS yuan basket index (.CFSCNYI), a gauge that measures the yuan against a basket of currencies, fell to 97.35 on Friday, the lowest since September 2023, according to Reuters calculations based on official data.

"The current U.S. tariff on China has reached 145%, and the impact of weakening the yuan against the dollar to support exports is only a drop in the bucket," said Ken Cheung, chief Asian FX strategist at Mizuho Bank.

"On the contrary, weakening the yuan against the basket could enhance the competitiveness of China's exports to non-U.S. countries."

Xing Zhaopeng, senior China strategist at ANZ, concurred, saying the yuan's value against the basket "needed to depreciate accordingly to reflect the changes" from higher U.S. tariffs on Chinese goods in order to make them competitive versus other countries.

Separately, the Hong Kong dollar hovered near a four-year high against the greenback on persistent inflows through the southbound leg of the stock trading link. It last traded at 7.7578 to the U.S. dollar around 0212 GMT, closer to the strong side of its trading band of 7.75.

data.title: 3D Energi Says Drill Rig Arrives on Site Ahead of Drilling
data.description:
3D Energi said the Transocean Equinox semi-submersible drilling rig arrived in the Otway Basin, along the southern coast of Australia, ahead of the Otway exploration drilling program, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Two firm exploration wells are scheduled for drilling this year, followed by up to four additional optional wells between 2026 and 2028, the filing said.

The rig is contracted for a minimum 16-well drilling campaign by a consortium of four operators, including ConocoPhillips Australia, the company's joint venture partner in the Otway Basin, per the filing.

data.title: Singapore Exchange Stands to Gain From Heavier Trading VolumeMarket Talk
data.description: Singapore Exchange stands to benefit from heavier-than-average trading volume on the local bourse, OCBC Investment Research's Research team says in a note. Several Straits Times Index component stocks have posted trading volumes exceeding 5X-6X their average six-month trading volume, the team says. Also, SGX will benefit from increased hedging activities for its derivatives business given heightened market volatility, the team says. Moreover, OCBC views management's focus on cost control as a positive amid current macroeconomic uncertainties. OCBC raises the stock's fair value estimate to S$14.09 from S$13.68 to reflect a rolling forward of valuations while maintaining the buy rating. Shares are 1.8% higher at S$12.78. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: FDI into the Philippines Drops 20% YoY in January
data.description:
Net foreign direct investment (FDI) in the Philippines dropped by 20% year-on-year to USD 0.7 billion in January 2025, primarily driven by a sharp decline in debt instruments (-37.7%).

Meanwhile, equity capital saw a reversal, jumping by 876.4%, while reinvestment of earnings (36%) also increased.

Equity capital placements for the month mainly came from Japan (48%), the United States (23%), Singapore (13%), and Malaysia (8%), with investments directed mostly to the manufacturing, financial and insurance, and real estate industries.

data.title: Giansun Precision Technology Raises 560 Million Yuan in Shenzhen IPO
data.description:
Giansun Precision Technology Group (SHE:001400) has priced its initial public offering on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange, seeking to raise 560.4 million yuan.

The Chinese equipment manufacturer is offering up to 15 million shares priced at 37.36 yuan apiece, according to a Friday filing on the Shenzhen bourse.

Subscriptions will begin on April 15 and the company will trade under stock code 001400.

data.title: Hong Kong stocks eye worst week since global financial crisis, China falls milder on state support
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks lost ground on Friday and were on track for their biggest weekly decline since the 2008 global financial crisis, battered as China takes the brunt of U.S. President Donald Trump's escalating trade war.

In mainland China, however, the stock market has been supported by purchases from state firms and the declines were much milder.

Thursday's relief rally as Trump hit the pause button on most of his so-called "reciprocal" tariffs was short-lived and traders are now assessing the impact of duties levied thus far. The pause did not include China which has had eye-popping duties of 145% levied on its goods this year.

The Hang Seng Index fell 0.8% in early Friday trade, hovering near a three-month low and bringing its losses this week to 9.7%. The tech subindex weakened 0.5%.

Beijing has stepped up efforts to stabilise mainland markets this week, with a state fund and state holding companies stepping into the market to buy stocks. A slew of listed firms have also announced share buybacks.

The Shanghai Composite Index was down just 0.1% on Friday morning, though the blue-chip CSI 300 Index dropped 0.6%. Both are on track to lose 3.8% this week, their worst weekly decline in four months.

Liam Zhou, founder of Shanghai-based Minority Asset Management, said his $1 billion portfolio is now fully invested in China stocks and that he was wagering on the country's economic resilience.

"China's retaliation against U.S. tariffs drew strength from the fact that China has reduced economic reliance on the U.S., it has proactively tamed property sector risks, made technology breakthroughs and is upgrading the manufacturing sector," he said.

Elsewhere, Wall Street saw a sharp decline overnight. MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) lost 0.3%, while Japan's Nikkei tumbled 4.2%.

data.title: U.S. Tariffs Set to Disrupt Corporate Supply Chains, Earnings in Asia-PacificMarket Talk
data.description: U.S. tariff hikes are set to disrupt corporate supply chains and earnings in Asia-Pacific despite the 90-day pause on some of the larger increases, says Fitch Ratings. Effective tariff rates on China have been raised further and aren't subject to the 90-day pause. This, coupled with the minimum 10% tariff on all APAC exports to the U.S., will hurt companies that sell to the U.S. and Asia's export-oriented economies, Fitch says in a note. The ratings firm sees the auto, tech hardware, chemicals and metals and mining sectors as most exposed to tariff risks. Once the April 2 tariff hikes are implemented in full, exports from Vietnam, Thailand, Indonesia, Taiwan, India, South Korea, Malaysia and Japan could face much higher tariffs, hurting growth and dimming corporate earnings prospects, Fitch adds. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

data.title: ABC-Mart Logs 13% Rise in Fiscal 2025 Profit as Sales Grow 8%
data.description:
ABC-Mart posted a 13.4% increase in profit attributable to owners of the parent to 45.4 billion yen for the fiscal year ended Feb. 28, up from 40 billion yen a year earlier, driven by stronger operating margins and cost management.

The footwear company's basic earnings per share rose to 183.18 yen from 161.58 yen last year, according to its Thursday bourse filing.

Net sales grew 8.1% to 372.2 billion yen, compared with 344.2 billion yen in the previous year, supported by domestic and overseas segment expansion.

For the fiscal year ending Feb. 28, 2026, the company expects attributable profit to gain 0.4% to 45.5 billion yen or 183.87 yen per share, while net sales are expected to rise 3.1% to 383.9 billion yen.

The company anticipates to pay interim and year-end dividends of 35 yen per share each, for the current fiscal year.

The company declared a year-end dividend of 37.00 yen per share for fiscal 2025, payable from May 28.

data.title: China's Export Growth Likely Picked Up in March,  Poll ShowsMarket Talk
data.description: China's outbound shipments were likely resilient in March despite U.S. tariffs, thanks partly to favorable base effects, according to a WSJ poll of economists. Chinese exports likely rose 4.4% on year, compared with the 2.3% increase in January-February, the poll shows. Beijing combines economic data for the first two months of the year to iron out distortions caused by shifts in the timing of the Lunar New Year holiday. Economists say the growth was aided by exporters' front-loading of shipments to get ahead of potentially higher tariffs. China's imports likely rose 1.8% last month, compared with the 8.4% drop in January-February. The country's March trade surplus is estimated at $77 billion versus $170.5 billion in January-February. The trade data are due Monday. (singaporeeditors@wsj.com)

data.title: Nomura's Profit Surges 75% in Fiscal 2025
data.description:
Nomura Co reported a 75% surge in profit attributable to owners of the parent to 6.76 billion yen for the fiscal year ended February 28, 2025, from 3.86 billion yen a year ago due to improved operational efficiency and strong demand in its core businesses.

The financial company's earnings per share (EPS) rose to 60.60 yen from 34.67 yen a year ago, according to a financial results filing.

Net sales jumped 12% to 150.3 billion yen from 134.1 billion yen a year earlier.

For the fiscal year ending Feb. 28, 2026, the company expects attributable profit to increase 3.6% to 7.0 billion yen or 62.75 yen per share, while net sales are expected to grow 3.2% to 155.0 billion yen.

The company expects to pay a year-end dividend of 34.00 yen per share for the current fiscal year.

Nomura shares shed nearly 5% recently.

data.title: KALENDER-Termine Politik/Vermischtes am Freitag, 11. April
data.description:
*************************************************************

Weitere ausführliche Terminübersichten erhalten sie unter:

* FIRMEN/MÄRKTE

* KONJUNKTUR/GELDPOLITIK

*************************************************************

Alle Zeitangaben in Central European Time (CET)

Immigration judge to rule in deportation case of Columbia University student and Palestinian activist - An immigration judge said she would rule on Friday on whether the U.S. government had made its case that Columbia University student and Palestinian activist Mahmoud Khalil should be deported. President Donald Trump's administration says it has revoked Khalil's status as a lawful permanent resident under a 1952 law allowing the deportation of any immigrant whose presence in the country the secretary of state deems harmful to U.S. foreign policy.

Russia's Lavrov attends CIS foreign ministers' meeting - Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov takes part in a meeting of CIS foreign ministers in Almaty.

Ukraine Defence Contact Group meet at NATO Headquarters - The United Kingdom and Germany to convene the Ukraine Defence Contact Group at NATO headquarters in Brussels.

Turkey hosts Antalya Diplomacy Forum, Syria's Sharaa and Russia's Lavrov to attend - Turkey hosts the Antalya Diplomacy Forum in the Mediterranean city of Antalya on April 11-13, with various foreign leaders attending including Syria's President Ahmed al-Sharaa and Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov. Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan will deliver a speech.

Spain's PM Sanchez to meet China's President Xi in Beijing - Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez to visit Beijing and meet with China's President Xi Jinping and Premier Li Qiang. Sanchez's visit to China comes as President Donald Trump's "reciprocal" tariffs on dozens of countries is expected to have taken effect on Wednesday, including massive 104% duties on Chinese goods, deepening his global trade war even as he prepared for negotiations with some nations.

09:00 - Chinese foreign ministry holds daily news conference - Chinese foreign ministry holds its daily news conference. Seeking comments as China 34% ‘reciprocal’ duties on U.S. products will kick in on April 10.

09:00 - Euro zone finance ministers to discuss impact of US tariffs on euro economy, crypto assets, Capital Markets Union, prepare IMF meetings - Finance ministers of the 20 countries sharing the euro will discuss the impact of U.S. tariffs on the euro zone economy, developments in crypto-asset activities and markets, prepare for the spring meetings of the International Monetary Fund, talks about exchange rate and inflation developments and discuss how to accelerate work on the Capital Markets Union (also called the European Savings and Investments Union)

20:30 - Texas judge to weigh extending block on Trump administration's deportations of Venezuelans under Alien Enemies Act - U.S. District Judge Fernando Rodriguez in Texas is due to weigh extending his block on the Trump administration's deportations of Venezuelans under the Alien Enemies Act.

data.title: Logitech Withdraws FY26 Guidance, Reiterates FY25 Outlook
data.description:
Logitech International said Thursday it withdrew its outlook for fiscal 2026 due to an uncertain tariff environment.

The Swiss computer peripherals and software company confirmed its forecast for fiscal 2025, expecting net sales of between $4.54 billion and $4.57 billion and non-GAAP operating income between $755 million and $770 million.
